label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage and see which question has not been discussed. First, the passage says that men have always believed they are smarter than women. Then a study shows that men are more in the top 2% and also more in the bottom 2%. The study's participants were tested on various subjects. The aggregate scores of men and women were similar. One of the authors, Professor Timothy Bates, suggests that men have been expected to be high achievers while women are restricted to home duties. He mentions the female developmental program may be tilted towards ensuring survival and the middle ground. The study aligns with past results where men are more likely to get first or third class degrees, while women get seconds. It also says men take more risks in academics while women are steadier. A past study showed women get more firsts and seconds, while men get more thirds. The argument is that coursework (which favors women's steady approach) is increasing over exams. Now, looking at the options: (A) Why are men smarter than women? - The passage says men are in the top 2% more than women, but it doesn't discuss why they are smarter; it just presents the fact. Also, it says the aggregate scores are similar. The study doesn't explain why men are smarter, just that they are more represented in extremes. So maybe this is the answer. (B) Why are men more stupid than women? - The passage says men are more in the bottom 2% and suggests reasons like expectations of being high achievers and taking more academic risks. So this is discussed. (C) How does the result go along with the past research? - The passage mentions that the research aligns with past results about university degrees and coursework vs exams. So this is discussed. (D) How can we help the men in the bottom? - The passage doesn't mention any solutions or ways to help men in the bottom. It just states the findings and possible reasons but no suggestions for help. So this might be the answer. Wait, but the question is which has NOT been discussed. So if (D) is not discussed, then it's the answer. But let me check again. Option (A) is about why men
